feedback.text: Student of the Jean Monnet Modules project “DERECHO”: ‘Defense of European Rights and Excellence for Consumers HHarmonisation’, No 101239437. Project financed by the European Union. Jean Monnet Actions in the field of Higher Education: Modules. Call for tenders ERASMUS-JMO-2025-HEI-TCH-RSCH. As a student of this project, I consider the European Union Digital Fairness Act to be a very interesting but above all relevant initiative when it comes to understanding how the law evolves to adapt to technological challenges. In my case, the examination and understanding of this legislation has done; first, to be aware of how justice and fairness are no longer limited to the physical sphere and, secondly, to understand how important it is to develop regulations that regulate and guarantee them also in the digital sphere. In person, understanding this legislation has made me aware of my own digital rights and the role of law in building a fair digital society. In short, the Digital Fair Act seems to me to be a very precise and necessary initiative today, and I strongly believe that as future lawyers, we have a responsibility to ensure that technology serves to extend rights, not to gene new forms of exclusion.
